title: Prison Break
overview:
“Prison Break” is a 2-4 player roll-and-write game with the objective being to be the first player to collect all four escape items from the prison in order to escape.

To collect these items, players need to collect reputation points and gang members to defeat the guards guarding these items.

“Prison Break” highlights key features of roll-and-write games, as a huge aspect of this game is that results are obtained from dice rolls, such as gang members, surveillance movement, general movement and guard tower actions.

The player keeps track of reputation points and gang members by marking off small stars or circles respectively on their game sheet.
contribution: The group worked together as a whole, developing the game together during each build session. I then wrote up the final document of the ruleset.
process:
This project focused on the iterative design, balancing, and playtesting of Prison Break, a prison-themed roll-and-write strategy game centred around resource management, risk assessment, and player interaction.

Players navigate a prison environment to build reputation, recruit gang members, overcome guard towers, and collect the resources required for escape, with progression driven by multiple interconnected mechanics and specialised dice systems.

Extensive playtesting and feedback sessions led to significant design improvements, including the introduction of player-versus-player fight blocks, increased punishment tile density, and adjusted progression requirements to improve pacing and strategic decision-making.

The game's design emphasised meaningful player choice, fairness through shared movement rolls, and thematic integration of all mechanics, while also identifying challenges such as complexity introduced by multiple die types and the Influence of luck on certain outcomes.
